story.title: I spent a week using the Trump phone — it sucks
story.summary: The Trump phone was never a serious phone. Not when it was announced last June, in dodgy renders and with an incoherent spec sheet. Nor when Trump Mobile admitted - just two weeks later - that it wouldn't be made in the US. Not even when the company revealed the final phone, first to me […]

points.heading: Not a Serious Phone
points.detail: The T1 phone is described as a marketing stunt, not a serious device, despite being a real product now on sale for $499.

points.heading: Cheap, Tacky Design
points.detail: The T1 is a curved slab of cheap gold plastic with sticky friction, and almost every detail speaks to bad design.

points.heading: Limited Network Support
points.detail: The phone cannot maintain signal stronger than 2G in the UK and likely will not work outside North America due to unsupported network bands.

points.heading: Underwhelming Performance
points.detail: Despite seemingly impressive specs, the T1 is often sluggish and stutters when switching apps, making even basic apps frustrating.
